local_dishes.name: Rösti (Swiss Potato Dish)
local_dishes.description: Crispy, golden potato base often served with cheese or vegetables—simple yet deeply satisfying

local_dishes.name: Butter Gipfeli (Swiss Croissant)
local_dishes.description: Flaky, buttery, and slightly denser than a French croissant—perfect with coffee for a quick breakfast or snack

local_dishes.name: Älplermagronen (Swiss Alpine Mac & Cheese — Veg Version)
local_dishes.description: A comforting alpine dish made with pasta, potatoes, cream, and cheese—rich, hearty, and very local to mountain regions

local_dishes.name: Vegetable Ravioli / Swiss-style Pasta
local_dishes.description: Fresh pasta dishes with seasonal vegetables and light sauces—common in Swiss-Italian influenced menus

local_dishes.name: Warm Alpine Vegetable Plates
local_dishes.description: Seasonal vegetables, grilled or sautéed, often served with local cheese or sauces—light yet flavourful
local_dishes.where_to_try: Available across all destinations, especially scenic cafés

local_dishes.name: Swiss Chocolate (Artisan Selections)
local_dishes.description: Smooth, high-quality chocolate—ranging from classic milk to rich dark varieties. A must-try while in Switzerland

local_dishes.name: Zuger Kirschtorte (Non-alcoholic versions available)
local_dishes.description: A layered cake made with sponge, cream, and cherries—light and delicate (ask for non-alcoholic variants)

local_dishes.name: Caramel Flan / Custard Desserts
local_dishes.description: Simple, creamy desserts commonly found across cafés and restaurants—great as a light sweet finish

local_dishes.name: Swiss Chocolate
local_dishes.description: High-quality chocolate brands like Lindt and Sprüngli
local_dishes.where_to_try: Confiserie Sprüngli in Zurich, or at chocolate shops across the country

local_dishes.name: Swiss Pastries (Nusstorte, Bündner Nusstorte)
local_dishes.description: A traditional Swiss nut tart, made with caramelized nuts and a buttery pastry
local_dishes.where_to_try: Engadine region, particularly in and around St. Moritz

local_dishes.name: Sausages (Bratwurst and Cervelat)
local_dishes.description: Swiss sausages often served with mustard, pickles, or in a bun

local_dishes.name: Bircher Müsli
local_dishes.description: A healthy Swiss breakfast made from oats, fruit, nuts, and yogurt, popular for its fresh and nutritious qualities

local_dishes.name: Swiss Pastries
local_dishes.description: From buttery, flaky croissants to sweet, jam-filled Nusstorte from Engadine

local_dishes.name: Swiss Cheese
local_dishes.description: Wide variety of cheeses including Emmental with its large holes, Gruyère, or Appenzeller with its strong flavor

local_dishes.name: Rivella
local_dishes.description: A popular Swiss drink made from whey and herbs, available in various flavors

local_dishes.name: Schümli Kaffee
local_dishes.description: A small, strong coffee served in a demitasse cup

local_dishes.name: Nusstorte
local_dishes.description: A traditional Swiss nut tart, made with caramelized nuts and a buttery pastry

safety_tips.tip: Keep your belongings secure, especially in busy areas and train stations
Be mindful of luggage while boarding trains or during transfers
Stick to well-lit areas if stepping out late
Avoid carrying large amounts of cash—cards are widely accepted
Wear proper warm layers—temperatures can drop quickly in mountains
Walk carefully on snowy or uneven paths
Check weather conditions before heading out to mountains
Stay hydrated, even in cold weather
Always follow pedestrian signals when crossing roads
Stay behind marked lines on train platforms
Trains are extremely punctual—avoid rushing last minute
Always greet with a "Grüezi" (in German-speaking regions)
Keep voices low on public transport
Tipping: ~5–10% in restaurants; round up for taxis
Always greet with a 'Grüezi' (in German-speaking regions)
Keep voices low on public transport
Tipping: ~5–10% in restaurants; round up for taxis

connectivity_tips.tip: You can purchase a TSIM Unlimited Europe SIM card which will work in both Switzerland and France
connectivity_tips._source_file: France/All Inclusive Guide- Switzerland and Paris.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: If you prefer, you can instead activate international roaming packs on your India number
connectivity_tips._source_file: France/All Inclusive Guide- Switzerland and Paris.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Even if you choose not to purchase an international roaming pack, do ensure your roaming is activated to receive SMS such as for OTPs
connectivity_tips._source_file: France/All Inclusive Guide- Switzerland and Paris.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: eSIM is the most convenient option - purchase from Airalo, Nomad, or Holafly online and activate before departure
Local Swiss SIM cards available from Swisscom (best coverage), Sunrise, or Salt at Zurich Airport and main stations
International roaming from Indian providers like Airtel and Jio is available but usually more expensive
Download Google Maps offline for all destinations before traveling
Use SBB Mobile App for real-time train schedules
Expect brief signal drops in tunnels and remote alpine areas
eSIM-Friendly Providers: Airalo or Nomad – regional Europe plans starting at ~$5
Paper SIMs (Local): TSIM
Use WhatsApp for calls. Free WiFi widely available in cities and trains
You can purchase a TSIM Unlimited Europe SIM card which will work in both Switzerland and France

connectivity_tips.tip: You can activate international roaming packs on your India number as an alternative

transport_options.description: All trains are free of cost with Swiss Pass. Boat tours at Interlaken on Lake Thun or Lake Brienz are also free
transport_options._source_file: France/All Inclusive Guide- Switzerland and Paris.pdf

transport_options.description: All trains including to Grindelwald and boats (lake cruises on Thun and Brienz) free of cost with Eurail pass
transport_options.cost: Mountain excursions and cable cars may need additional ticket with discount

transport_options.description: Extensive rail network connecting major cities and tourist destinations
transport_options.cost: CHF 25-225 depending on route and destination
transport_options._source_file: Switzerland/All Inclusive Guide Switzerland and Austria - Omkar.pdf

transport_options.description: All-in-one ticket for seamless travel across Switzerland covering unlimited train travel, buses, trams, lake cruises, and discounts on mountain excursions
Extremely punctual and scenic train network connecting all major cities and mountain destinations
Mountain transport to alpine destinations like Jungfraujoch, Gornergrat, and Grindelwald First
Scenic boat rides on Lake Lucerne and Lake Zurich
All trains are free of cost with Swiss Pass. Boat tours at Interlaken are also free. Mountain excursions and cable cars may require additional tickets but with discounts
transport_options._source_file: Switzerland/All Inclusive Guide- Switzerland and Paris.pdf

transport_options.description: Comprehensive pass covering trains, buses, and cable cars throughout Switzerland
transport_options._source_file: Switzerland/All Inclusive Guide - Switzerland and Austria - Omkar.pdf

transport_options.description: Regional train service connecting cities and airports
Mountain transport to peaks like Mt. Titlis and Harder Kulm
Lake cruises on Lake Thun and Lake Brienz
Bus service connecting remote villages and mountain areas
All trains are free of cost with Swiss Pass, boat tours at Interlaken on Lake Thun or Lake Brienz are also free

transport_options.description: Extensive rail network connecting major cities and tourist destinations
transport_options._source_file: Austria/All Inclusive Guide - Switzerland and Austria - Omkar.pdf

health_tips.tip: Tap water is safe to drink across all cities and mountain regions
At high-altitude locations like Jungfraujoch and Gornergrat, you may feel slight breathlessness—move at a comfortable pace
Sun exposure is stronger in the mountains—carry sunscreen (SPF 50), sunglasses, and lip balm
Carry basic medications including personal prescriptions, cold & flu relief, motion sickness tablets, and a small first-aid kit
Travel insurance is highly recommended to cover medical needs, delays, or unforeseen situations
Ensure regular travel vaccines are up to date: Tetanus, Hepatitis A (recommended), Measles/MMR booster
Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
Bring hand sanitizer for rural areas and public transport
Ensure regular travel vaccines are up to date: Tetanus, Hepatitis A (recommended), Measles/MMR booster
Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
Bring hand sanitizer for rural areas and public transport
